title: quercus glauca var. salicina
intro: Quercus glauca var. salicina, also known as the Japanese evergreen oak or evergreen Quercus, is a plant species that belongs to the family Fagaceae. It has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine, particularly in China and Japan, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The leaves of Quercus glauca var. salicina are believed to have various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves are typically dried and made into tea, or used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicinal formulas.
